All applicants must have a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.4.</p><p><br></p><p>The Bioengineering curriculum focuses on five thrust ideas: molecular, cellular and tissue engineering, biomedical devices and bioinstrumentation, biomedical imaging, biomedical data sciences and neural engineering.</p><p><br></p><p>Admission to the Henry Samueli School of Engineering and Applied Science (HSSEAS) at UCLA is highly competitive. The most important selection criteria are completion of the required preparatory courses and academic performance. All applicants must have a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.4.</p><p><br></p><p>To be considered for admission to this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer:</p><ul><li>One and a half years of Calculus through Multivariable,</li><li>One course in Linear Algebra,</li><li>One course in Differential Equations,</li><li>One year of General Chemistry with Labs,</li><li>One year of Organic Chemistry,</li><li>One and a half years of Calculus Based Physics series [for Engineering majors] with Lab, and</li><li>One course in Java, C, C++ or MATLAB.</li></ul><p>One year of biology with lab for the major (cellular, molecular, and genetic) is <strong>strongly recommended.</strong></p><p>All courses must be taken for a letter grade.</p><p><br></p><p>HSSEAS admits students by major and does not consider applicants for alternate majors. Applicants are not required to complete the HSSEAS General Education Requirements in order to be admitted, although it is beneficial for students to complete 1 course from each of the following areas: arts, humanities, social sciences, and life sciences.</p><p>Partial IGETC is NOT accepted.</p><p><br></p><p>For more information regarding IGETC acceptance, this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, please visit <u style=\"color: rgb(41, 98, 255);\">https://</u><a href=\"http://www.admission.ucla.edu/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">admission.ucla.edu</a>.
